title: Some users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365
impact: Users may have experienced increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365.

updates.content:
Title: Some users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365

User impact: Users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365.

More info: This issue specifically impacts users who utilize the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices.
 
For more details regarding the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method, please reference the following documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/rdp-shortpath?tabs=managed-networks

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with some users experiencing increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365, and we're checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Some users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365

User impact: Users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365.

More info: This issue specifically impacts users who utilize the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices.
 
For more details regarding the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method, please reference the following documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/rdp-shortpath?tabs=managed-networks

Current status: We've identified that a recent service update introduced a configuration issue which is resulting in impact. We're reverting the offending service update, which we anticipate will complete by our next scheduled communications update.

Scope of impact: Some users utilizing the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365 may be impacted.

Start time: Monday, April 20, 2026, at 6:00 PM UTC

Root cause: A recent service update introduced a configuration issue, resulting in impact.

Next update time: Thursday, April 23, 2026, at 10:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365

User impact: Users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365.

More info: This issue specifically impacts users who utilize the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices.
 
For more details regarding the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method, please reference the following documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/rdp-shortpath?tabs=managed-networks

Current status: We're monitoring the reversion of the offending service update which continues to make progress but is taking longer than initially anticipated. We'll aim to provide a timeline for the reversion to complete by our next scheduled communications update.

Scope of impact: Some users utilizing the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365 may be impacted.

Start time: Monday, April 20, 2026, at 6:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365

User impact: Users may have experienced increased latency on their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365.

More info: This issue specifically impacted users who utilize the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices.
 
For more details regarding the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method, please reference the following documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/rdp-shortpath?tabs=managed-networks

Final status: We've confirmed that our reversion of the problematic service update has completed and verified through internal monitoring that impact has been successfully remediated.

Scope of impact: Some users utilizing the RDP Shortpath (TURN) connection method to access their Cloud PC devices launched through Windows 365 may have been impacted.

Start time: Monday, April 20, 2026, at 6:00 PM UTC

End time: Thursday, April 23, 2026, at 11:00 PM UTC

Root cause: A recent service update introduced a configuration issue, resulting in impact.

Next steps: We're reviewing our update testing and validation methods to better identify potential configuration issues such as this prior to deployment so we can prevent similar impact in the future. 

This is the final update for the event.
